business.headline: Stock market crash: Why you should not stop your mutual fund SIPs in panicWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

Japan first G7 nation to announce release of oil reserves amid Gulf conflictThe move comes as global energy markets remain on edge due to escalating tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which has raised fears of disruptions to crude oil shipments from the Middle East.

IEA may recommend release of strategic oil reserves amid Iran war concernsThe proposed release could exceed 100 million barrels of oil over the first month, the sources said, in what would be one of the largest coordinated stockpile releases aimed at stabilising global oil markets and preventing a sharp spike in crude prices.

Dalal Street in panic: Sensex crashes 1,300 points, Nifty falls below 23,900The BSE Sensex closed 1,342 points lower, while the Nifty 50 slipped below the key 23,900 level by the end of the trading session.

Will costlier oil weaken the rupee and widen India's trade deficit?India consumes roughly 5.3 to 5.5 million barrels of crude oil every day. However, domestic production is only about 0.6 million barrels per day. This means the country depends on imports for nearly 85% of its oil needs.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Sensex and Nifty fell sharply after opening higher as banking and auto stocks dragged the market down. So what triggered the sudden sell-off, and what should investors watch now?

Live: Delivery Code System being implemented amid 25-day LPG cylinder waitLPG Shortage Live Updates: Concerns over a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders have surfaced across several Indian cities, with restaurants and hotels reporting disruptions in gas supply over the past few days. Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, government response, city-wise impact and supply situation across India.

India eases FDI rules for neighbouring countries: What the new 10% rule meansThe decision was approved by the Union Cabinet and the changes are intended to boost foreign investment, help startups access global funding and support manufacturing growth in India.

Why Reliance Industries shares are gaining in early trade todayAt around 9:23 a.m., RIL shares were trading at Rs 1,427.20, up Rs 18.40 or 1.31% from the previous close of Rs 1,408.80. The stock opened at Rs 1,424.90 and touched an intraday high of Rs 1,434.00.

Sensex, Nifty trade flat in early session; Reliance gains, banks weighAt around 9:18 a.m., the BSE Sensex was at 78,279.66, up 73.68 points or 0.09%. The Nifty 50 stood at 24,256.90, down 4.70 points or 0.02% after touching an intraday high of 24,292.35.

Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

G7 stops short of releasing oil reserves, asks IEA to assess market optionsAfter soaring to a more than three-year high in the previous session, oil prices plummeted 7 per cent on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump predicted the war in the Middle East could end soon, easing concerns about prolonged disruptions to oil supplies.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers steps down 3 months after operational meltdownThe resignation comes after the airline faced its worst-ever operational crisis in December, which drew scrutiny from both the public and the government.

Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringThe Iran-US conflict has disrupted shipping through a key global oil export route, pushing oil prices higher. As a result, airline tickets on some routes have become much more expensive, and there are growing concerns that travel demand could weaken if the situation continues.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

entertainment.headline: Ravi Mohan reacts to criticism amid divorce case: Men are the biggest victims Actor Ravi Mohan responded to criticism over his marital issues, sharing a message on social media about his perspective on men's experiences in marital conflicts.

Charithra Chandran of One Piece Season 2 says, proud to be brown and Indian Charithra Chandran speaks exclusively to India Today about the significance of her casting as a win for South Asian visibility, sharing her journey as a proud Indian actor in a global franchise.

Mona Singh says she was mindblown by Gaurav Gera's transformation in Dhurandhar Mona Singh expressed pride over her Jassi Jaissi Koi Nahin co-star Gaurav Gera receiving widespread praise for his role as Aalam Bhai in Dhurandhar.

Dhurandhar's Rakesh Bedi reacts on Toxic being postponed due to US-Iran conflict As Dhurandhar: The Revenge gets ready for release on March 19, actor Rakesh Bedi, who returns as Jameel Jamali, notes that the film has not been postponed by the US-Iran conflict, unlike Toxic. He also addresses reports that suggest his character is based on Pakistani politician Nabil Gabol.

'Stop with this brain rot': Sona Mohapatra slams Badshah's song Tateeree amid row Singer Sona Mohapatra called out Badshah's latest song, Tateeree, for reinforcing harmful stereotypes and objectifying women. Following widespread criticism, the song was taken off all platforms.

Director Harish Shankar on Ustaad Bhagat Singh vs Dhurandhar 2: No competition As Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh are set to release on the same date, director Harish Shankar has reacted to the box office clash.

Daniel Radcliffe asks for mandatory therapy for children on film sets Daniel Radcliffe said therapy should be mandatory for child actors on film sets. Hinted that the upcoming Harry Potter series may introduce stronger mental health support.

Prithviraj Sukumaran, Manju Warrier to star in Rahul Sadasivan's horror film: Report Malayalam filmmaker Rahul Sadasivan, known for acclaimed horror titles like Bhoothakaalam and Bramayugam, is reportedly preparing another supernatural drama. The film reportedly features Prithviraj Sukumaran and Manju Warrier.

Why did Priyanka Chopra move on from Bollywood? She 'doesn't like staying in s**t' Priyanka Chopra opened up on the reason behind leaving Bollywood at the peak of her acting career. She also called Aishwarya Rai and Mindy Kaling her inspiration in Hollywood.

Ek Din trailer: Junaid Khan rings a fortune bell to win Sai Pallavi's love The trailer of Ek Din, starring Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi, is out. Directed by Sunil Pandey, the movie is slated to release in theatres on May 1.

Actor Lavanya Tripathi files cybercrime complaint over online abuse Actor Lavanya Tripathi has filed a cybercrime complaint with Hyderabad Police, alleging harassment and abusive content targeting her and her family on social media platforms. A case has been registered, and an investigation is underway.

Dhurandhar's Shararat singer Madhubanti on why she was hesitant to appear in video Singer Madhubanti Bagchi shared that she was initially hesitant to appear in the music video of the chartbuster Shararat from Dhurandhar, saying being on camera is outside her comfort zone.

Priyanka Chopra credits film with Thalapathy Vijay for her first taste of fame During a recent podcast, Priyanka Chopra shared her experience of working with Thalapathy Vijay in her debut film Thamizhan, recalling how popular the Leo actor was at the time and how his fame made her curious about what it felt like to be that famous.

Hansika Motwani, Sohael Khaturiya granted divorce; no alimony involved Actor Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya have ended their four-year marriage. A family court in Mumbai has granted the couple a divorce.

Veteran Tamil filmmaker Thakkali Srinivasan dies at 72 in Bengaluru Filmmaker and musician Thakkali Srinivasan, recognised for his contributions to Tamil and Telugu cinema, died in Bengaluru at the age of 72. His last rites took place on March 11.

Gwyneth Paltrow made big deal of massage comment, nothing happened: Harvey Weinstein Harvey Weinstein said that Gwyneth Paltrow "owes her career" to him. He dismissed her statement about sexual misconduct, alleging that she stabbed him in the back when "nothing had happened."

Ustaad Bhagat Singh director apologises to Mahesh Babu fans. Here's why After his reply to a post went viral and drew backlash from Mahesh Babu's fans, director Harish Shankar issued a clarification, calling it unintentional. He explained that he liked an X post without fully reading it.

Anurag Kashyap on mention in Epstein files: Wow! What a company I have Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap responded to a reference to the name of a "famous Bollywood director" in records related to Jeffrey Epstein. In an interaction, Kashyap clarified that the reference does not concern him.

Do SRK, Big B, Akshay have huge entourage? Taapsee Pannu reveals the facts Taapsee Pannu addressed the ongoing debate about rising Bollywood entourage costs, saying it is a myth that every star travels with a large team.

general news.headline: Opposition's no-trust motion against Speaker defeated in Lok Sabha amid uproarAfter a charged debate and protests in the House, the Lok Sabha rejected the opposition's no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla by voice vote.

Thai cargo ship bound for Gujarat attacked in Strait of Hormuz, 20 crew rescuedA cargo vessel bound for Gujarat caught fire after being hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting an Omani navy rescue that evacuated most of the crew.

Rahul Gandhi in Germany, England during key debates: Amit Shah's all-out attackWhile speaking in Parliament on the no-trust motion against Lok Sabha speaker Om Bira, Shah emphasised that the motion has severe shortcomings.

Two Indians killed, one missing after merchant ship attack in Iran war: MEAMEA spokesperson, Randhir Jaiswal, said the casualties occurred when the Indians were aboard commercial ships that were targeted. The MEA also confirmed that a number of Indians injured in the Gulf region are receiving medical treatment.

God is great. So is the other God. Also, the other oneThe current war in the Middle East isn't being fought for oil or money or power. All the parties involved, Iran, the US and Israel, are utterly convinced that it is a divine task and the Almighty Himself is on their side.

Live: Opposition demands apology after Amit Shah's attendance jab at Rahul GandhiBudget Session Live Updates: The debate on the resolution seeking the removal of Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla is expected to conclude today, with Union Home Minister Amit Shah set to respond to the motion. Earlier on Tuesday, the ruling NDA strongly defended Birla, while opposition MPs accused him of partisanship and restricting Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi from raising key issues in the House. Treasury bench members rejected the allegations, saying action against opposition MPs was taken due to their conduct. Follow indiatoday.in for updates.

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

Live: Israel launches fresh attack on Tehran; toll rises to 570 in LebanonUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the joint front of Israel and the United States entered its 12th day on Wednesday, with fresh Iranian missile attacks targeting central Israel before dawn as air raid sirens sounded, and Israeli air defences intercepted incoming rockets. Iranian state media also reported new strikes on US military installations in Bahrain early Wednesday, following a wave of Iranian missile and drone attacks late Tuesday on bases linked to the US in Qatar, Iraq's Kurdistan, the UAE and Bahrain. The escalation comes after intense US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran on Tuesday, described by the Pentagon and witnesses as among the heaviest of the war so far. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned it could block oil shipments from the Gulf unless the attacks stop, raising fears of disruption to global energy supplies. Meanwhile, the White House reiterated US President Donald Trump's warning of severe retaliation if Iran attempts to halt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, even as he said US forces had destroyed 10 Iranian 'inactive' mine-laying vessels in recent strikes. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East war.

Meet India's high-protein 'designer rice' built to fight diabetesScientists in India have developed a new type of rice that contains three times more protein and has a lower glycaemic index. The innovation could help tackle diabetes, malnutrition, and nutrient deficiencies without changing people's eating habits.

Live: Delivery Code System being implemented amid 25-day LPG cylinder waitLPG Shortage Live Updates: Concerns over a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders have surfaced across several Indian cities, with restaurants and hotels reporting disruptions in gas supply over the past few days. Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, government response, city-wise impact and supply situation across India.

Trump announces historic $300 billion oil refinery in Texas, thanks RelianceAnnouncing the project in a Truth Social post, Trump said the refinery will be built in Brownsville, Texas, as part of a USD 300 billion deal aimed at boosting American energy production and strengthening economic ties with international partners.

Iran's readout of Jaishankar call reveals more about India ties than it saysDespite India's close ties with Israel and the US, Iran continues to maintain a restrained and pragmatic approach towards New Delhi, recognising the strategic value of preserving a long-standing partnership built on energy ties and geopolitical balance.

Hansika Motwani, Sohael Khaturiya granted divorce; no alimony involvedActor Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya have ended their four-year marriage. A family court in Mumbai has granted the couple a divorce.

Japan first G7 nation to announce release of oil reserves amid Gulf conflictThe move comes as global energy markets remain on edge due to escalating tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which has raised fears of disruptions to crude oil shipments from the Middle East.

2 meals a day, no dosa-poori: Bengaluru PG body's guidelines amid LPG crunchBengaluru PG accommodations have begun restricting meal options and limiting gas-heavy dishes like dosa, poori and chapati due to a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. PG owners have advised switching to rice-based dishes, reducing weekday meals to two, and using electric cooking alternatives.

Induction cooktops going out of stock across India amid LPG crisisA shortage in LPG cylinders across India has triggered a sharp surge in demand for induction cooktops. Quick commerce platforms and retailers in several neighbourhoods across Mumbai, Delhi and Bengaluru are running out of stock. Retailers report selling 50 to 70 units of induction stoves a day amid panic-buying.

Vijay Deverakonda may be the best-dressed celebrity groom India has seenVijay Deverakonda did something most celebrity grooms rarely do: he treated his wardrobe like a narrative, not just ceremonial dressing.

This IIT Delhi wind tunnel turns Bernoulli's Principle into a breezeThe 'Wind Tunnel', yes, you read that right, at IIT Delhi is often referred to by students as a real-life example of Bernoulli's Principle, a concept they usually study in physics textbooks.

New Supreme Leader injured in war but safe, says Iran President's sonPezeshkian said that despite the injuries, the new Supreme Leader, who replaced his father, Ali Khamenei, after his killing in the US-Israel strikes, is "safe and sound".

Woman removed from flight for playing video aloud, meltdown caught on cameraA woman was escorted off an American Airlines flight from Miami to Tampa after refusing to use headphones while playing a video on her phone.

India's cheese story is changing and mozzarella isn't the star anymorePeople today are taking a break from processed cheese and looking beyond Western varieties to experiment with local artisanal cheeses from different regions.

India now has over 50 CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

Supreme Court allows passive euthanasia for Harish Rana in historic verdictThe court ruled that clinically assisted nutrition qualifies as medical treatment and can be withdrawn if medical boards recommend it, reiterating that the key test is whether continuing treatment serves the patient's best interest.

Air India Express jet stuck on Phuket runway after losing nose wheel during landingThe aircraft's nose landing gear was damaged during the landing, but no injuries were reported. Airport operations were temporarily suspended to ensure safety and allow inspection and clearance of the runway.

sports.headline: IPL 2026 schedule live: Fixtures and venues of first 20 games out today IPL 2026 schedule announcement live: The Indian Premier League 2026 schedule is all set to be announced on March 11. The tournament fixtures will be unveiled in phases due to state elections in several parts of the country.

Dhoni never asked to drop Yuvraj Singh: Ex-BCCI selector sets record straight Ex-BCCI chief selector Sandeep Patil has gone on record to say that MS Dhoni never once asked for Yuvraj Singh to be dropped from the Indian team, contradicting Yograj Singh's long-held claims.

No Babar Azam and Pakistan slip to new low in first match after T20 World Cup PAK vs BAN, 1st ODI: Without Babar Azam in their lineup, Pakistan slumped to their lowest ODI total against Bangladesh when the two teams met on Wednesday, March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur. Bangladesh chased the target with 209 deliveries remaining.

ICC awards Rs 24 crore prize money to India for T20 World Cup 2026 triumph The International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced Rs 24 crore prize money for the Indian cricket team for winning the T20 World Cup 2026. India beat New Zealand in the final to clinch their third title.

New Zealand Women spinner goes past Viv Richards to cap off dream series NZW vs ZIMW: Amelia Kerr enjoyed a dream ODI series against Zimbabwe, taking 16 wickets from three matches at an economy rate of 4.07. Earlier in the limited-overs leg of the tour, the New Zealand skipper also claimed a seven-wicket haul and scored her maiden T20I century.

Suryakumar, Shivam Dube felicitated by Maharashtra CM Fadnavis for T20 World Cup win T20 World Cup 2026-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav and Shivam Dube were felicitated by Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis at Varsha Bungalow in Mumbai.

MS Dhoni will play full IPL 2026 season, can't talk about his role: CSK CEO IPL 2026: MS Dhoni, 44, will play the entirety of the upcoming season, Chennai Super Kings CEO Kasi Viswanathan said on Wednesday, March 11. The upcoming edition of the IPL is likely to get underway on March 28.

Will Bumrah focus more on white-ball cricket than Tests? Glenn McGrath reacts Glenn McGrath wonders if Jasprit Bumrah will continue playing Test cricket and will switch his focus to white-ball formats. Bumrah was the star of the show as India defeated New Zealand in the final to win the T20 World Cup.

Tell me what I can do? Surya reveals Samson's inspiring mid-World Cup question T20 World Cup: India captain Suryakumar Yadav revealed that Sanju Samson had asked what he could do for the team despite starting the 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup on the bench. Samson later emerged as India's biggest hero of the tournament.

F1's return in India gathers pace as sports minister meets motorsports body Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya met with the FMSCI to discuss reviving Formula One in India, exploring a framework to build a pipeline of drivers and technicians.

Varun Chakravarthy dethroned as No.1 T20 bowler after poor World Cup T20 World Cup: Varun Chakravarthy's reign at the top of the ICC T20I bowlers rankings has come to an end after his lacklustre performance during the second phase of the T20 World Cup 2026.

T20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan top ICC rankings, Samson makes big leapIshan Kishan has climbed to No.2 in the ICC T20I batter rankings, while Sanju Samson jumps 18 spots to 22nd with a career-best 637 points after India's T20 World Cup triumph.

Kirti Azad defends remarks on Team India's temple visit: Everyone should be respectedFormer India cricketer Kirti Azad has defended his criticism of Team India for taking the T20 World Cup 2026 trophy to a temple, as head coach Gautam Gambhir hit back at the remarks.

Donald Trump has given Iran green light to play FIFA World Cup in USA: InfantinoFIFA president Gianni Infantino said US President Donald Trump had assured that Iran would be allowed to travel to the United States to compete in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The confirmation came amid growing doubts over Iran's participation due to rising geopolitical tensions.

Thought it was a dig at Ro-Ko: Ex-ENG star on Gambhir's T20 WC final commentsFormer England pacer Steve Harmison believes Gautam Gambhir's post-T20 World Cup remarks targeted senior Indian batters Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli. Gambhir said he wants to create a culture in Indian cricket where the focus is on the team's success rather than personal milestones.

Virat Kohli's preparations begin, hits the nets ahead IPL 2026: WatchVirat Kohli has been spotted hitting the nets ahead of IPL 2026, with the Indian batting great putting in the hard yards early as defending champions RCB gear up for the new season.

Gambhir responds to MS Dhoni's viral post, explains why he doesn't smile in dugoutGautam Gambhir responded to MS Dhoni's viral smile remark after India's T20 World Cup win and explained why he chooses to keep a serious face during matches. Dhoni's special message to Gambhir after the T20 World Cup win went viral.

India's next T20 dynasty: Sanju Samson and Co carry Rohit-Kohli's legacy forwardTwo years after Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Ravindra Jadeja stepped away from T20Is, India's new core delivered the perfect answer. Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan, Axar Patel and Shivam Dube carried the legacy forward with a title-winning run at the 2026 T20 World Cup.

PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi stranded in Qatar amidst ongoing West Asia crisisPSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi has been stranded in Qatar for over a week due to West Asia war flight disruptions, as the club prepares to host Chelsea in the Champions League.

Sen in Zen mode: Lakshya swaps the sprint for a steady, mature ascentLakshya Sen's All England 2026 final appearance was bittersweet, yet it highlighted the evolution of his game and mindset. After the pain of Paris Olympics disappointment, the 24-year-old has shifted into a calmer, Zen-like phase, prioritising recovery and mental strength over the relentless grind for ranking points.

Jasprit Bumrah to focus more on ODIs after T20 World Cup triumphJasprit Bumrah is set to prioritise ODIs over T20Is as India begin planning for the 2027 World Cup. The BCCI and team management will carefully manage his workload in the upcoming cycle.

How Bumrah's unusual action created cricket's most dangerous slower ballsJasprit Bumrah was once again at the centre of the limelight in India's T20 World Cup 2026 win. At India Today, we explore the origin story of the man who coached him in his early days, and why his unorthodox action was never changed.

Gambhir slams Kirti Azad after T20 World Cup remark: Degrading your own playersIndia coach Gautam Gambhir slammed Kirti Azad's comment about the team taking the T20 World Cup trophy to the Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad, saying it was degrading the players and their achievement on the day.

7 Iranian women footballers receive asylum in Australia during West Asia tensionsSeven members of Iran's women's soccer team were granted humanitarian visas in Australia after seeking asylum, while the rest of the squad returned home following protests and concerns over safety amid the Iran war.

T20 World Cup medal in hand, Shivam Dube reveals how he returned home via trainT20 World Cup: Just hours after helping India win the T20 World Cup, Shivam Dube quietly boarded a train from Ahmedabad to Mumbai. With flights full, the all-rounder chose the fastest route home to see his children.

Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill was great T20 World Cup call by Gambhir: HarbhajanT20 World Cup: Harbhajan Singh praised Gautam Gambhir and the selectors for backing Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill at the T20 World Cup. Kishan repaid that faith with a breakout campaign as India lifted the title.

SA, WI travel decisions made on safety: ICC rejects bias claimsT20 World Cup: The ICC on Wednesday, March 11 said that the South Africa and West Indies teams will leave India within the next 36 hours. The international cricket body also rejected claims that they showed any bias towards both sides during the travel fiasco that has seen them get stranded in India.

FC Barcelona and Liverpool stunned on UCL return, Atletico thrash clueless SpursBarcelona and Liverpool stumbled on hostile away nights in the Champions League Round of 16 first legs. Atletico Madrid punished Tottenham brutally, while Bayern Munich delivered a ruthless six-goal statement against Atalanta.

ODI World Cup, WTC wins bigger than T20 World Cup: Manjrekar odd take goes viralFormer cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has gone viral over his odd take on the T20 World Cup. Manjrekar feels that ODI World Cup and the WTC wins are bigger achievements than winning the T20 World Cup.

England right-back Kyle Walker retires 4 games short of 100 international capsKyle Walker has announced his retirement from international football, bringing the curtain down on a 14-year England career. Walker played for England 96 times and appeared at five major tournaments.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
